content: Comparative analyses of intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning can be reframed to benchmark how different intrinsic reward signals interact with the 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory architectures to shape long-horizon episodic planning capability. We hypothesize that RND’s error-based novelty bonus, unlike ICM’s prediction error intrinsic reward, disproportionately boosts the performance of sparse, retrieval-augmented agent memory by rewarding exploration of under-encoded memory slots rather than just ambiguous state predictions.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
